summary: Social media web app with user management, posts/feed, stories/reels, likes/comments, subscriptions, and notifications using Internet Computer
architectural_notes: Backend: Motoko canisters on Internet Computer for user/post/subscription management
Frontend: React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS for responsive web UI
Authentication: Internet Identity with passkeys and OAuth (Google, Apple, Microsoft)
Storage: IC canisters for small media; IPFS integration for large videos
Payments: Stripe checkout via Node.js server, webhook updates IC canister
Media handling: Store URLs/CIDs in canister, not full files for large content
Notifications: Polling or external WebSocket relay for real-time updates

existing_features.title: Create frontend PostCard component that displays post content, media, author information, like count, and like button with state management
Create frontend Home page that fetches and displays feed of all posts using React Query, with infinite scroll or pagination
Create frontend Profile page that displays user information (username, email, subscription status) and user's posts
Create frontend Subscribe page with Stripe integration that initiates checkout session and updates user subscription status in backend upon successful payment
Create frontend StoryViewer component with carousel navigation that displays stories with media content and auto-advance timer
Create frontend ReelPlayer component using HTML5 video tag that plays video content with controls, loop functionality, and like/comment interface overlay
Create frontend Navbar component with navigation links to Home, Profile, and Subscribe pages, plus authentication status indicator

Design a cohesive visual theme with warm, energetic colors (oranges, yellows, corals), modern typography with rounded sans-serif fonts, and card-based layouts with subtle shadows and smooth transitions throughout the application

new_feature_requests.summary: User authentication with Internet Identity (passkeys, Google, Apple, Microsoft OAuth)
User profile management (username, email, subscription status)
Post creation with text and media upload
Feed view with posts from all users
Like/unlike functionality for posts
Comment system for posts
Stories feature with viewer carousel
Reels/video player functionality
Stripe subscription payment integration
Notification system (polling-based or WebSocket relay)
Implement User data model in Motoko backend with fields: id (Principal), username (Text), email (Text), and subscription (Bool)
Implement Post data model in Motoko backend with fields: id (Nat), author (Principal), content (Text), mediaUrl (Text), likes (array of Principal), and createdAt (Nat timestamp)
Create backend function createPost that accepts author (Principal), content (Text), and mediaUrl (Text), generates unique post ID, stores post in canister state, and returns the created Post
Create backend function likePost that accepts postId (Nat) and user (Principal), adds user to post's likes array if not already present, and prevents duplicate likes from same user
Create backend function getFeed that returns array of all posts sorted by creation time (newest first)
Create backend function subscribeUser that accepts user Principal and sets their subscription status to true
Implement backend data model for Stories with fields: id (Nat), author (Principal), mediaUrl (Text), expiresAt (Nat timestamp), and views (array of Principal)
Implement backend data model for Comments with fields: id (Nat), postId (Nat), author (Principal), content (Text), and createdAt (Nat timestamp)
Create backend function addComment that accepts postId (Nat), author (Principal), and content (Text), stores comment, and returns created Comment object
Create backend function getComments that accepts postId (Nat) and returns array of all comments for that post sorted by creation time

Implement notification tracking in backend with data model containing: id (Nat), recipientId (Principal), type (Text for like/comment/follow), relatedId (Nat), senderId (Principal), read (Bool), and createdAt (Nat timestamp)
Create backend function getNotifications that accepts userId (Principal) and returns array of user's notifications sorted by creation time (newest first)
